name: AA Intelligence Index
description: Artificial Analysis composite across 10 sub-benchmarks (GPQA Diamond, HLE, SciCode, Terminal-Bench Hard, τ²-Bench Telecom, AA-LCR, AA-Omniscience, GDPval-AA, IFBench, CritPt).

variants.label: Default
variants.notes: AA's published composite on a 0–100 scale. Current frontier around 57 (Opus 4.7).

name: AA Coding Index
description: AA composite across coding-heavy benchmarks (LiveCodeBench, SciCode, Terminal-Bench Hard). 0–100.

variants.label: Default
variants.notes: Average of coding sub-benchmarks, normalized to 0–100.

name: AA Math Index
description: AA composite of math benchmarks (AIME 2025, MATH-500). 0–100.

variants.label: Default
variants.notes: Math composite. Often null for models AA hasn't re-evaluated against the current math suite.

name: MMLU-Pro
description: Harder, deduplicated variant of MMLU across 14 domains.

variants.label: Default (0-shot CoT)
variants.notes: Zero-shot with chain-of-thought. AA dropped this from the Intelligence Index but still reports per-model scores.

name: GPQA Diamond
description: Graduate-level science (bio/physics/chem) — diamond subset, 198 questions.

variants.label: Default
variants.notes: AA's standard 0-shot run on the diamond subset.

variants.label: Deep Think
variants.notes: Vendor extended-reasoning mode (e.g. Google Deep Think).

name: Humanity's Last Exam
description: 2,500 expert-authored questions across mathematics, humanities and the natural sciences. Intentionally hard — frontier models still under 45%.

variants.label: Default (no tools)
variants.notes: No tool use, no web. Single-shot. What AA publishes.

variants.label: With tools
variants.notes: Model has browsing + code execution. Not comparable to default — reported scores typically much higher.

variants.label: Deep Think
variants.notes: Vendor's extended-reasoning mode (e.g. Google Deep Think).

name: MATH-500
description: 500-problem subset of the MATH benchmark (competition-style).

variants.label: Default (pass@1)
variants.notes: Single sample. AA reports null for many 2026 frontier models (rerun pending).

name: AIME 2025
description: American Invitational Mathematics Examination 2025 — 30 problems.

variants.label: Default
variants.notes: AA reports a single default score per model (their `aime_25` field).

name: LiveCodeBench
description: Contamination-controlled coding benchmark from recent competitive-programming problems.

variants.label: pass@1 (no tools)
variants.notes: Single sample, no execution feedback. AA default.

name: SciCode
description: Scientific coding benchmark across physics, chemistry, biology and math. Still in AA's current Intelligence Index.

name: IFBench
description: Instruction-Following Bench — tests adherence to complex, multi-part instructions. In AA's current Intelligence Index.

name: AA-LCR
description: Long-Context Reasoning benchmark authored by AA. Part of the current Intelligence Index.

name: Terminal-Bench Hard
description: Hard subset of Terminal-Bench — agent executes real shell commands against a sandboxed filesystem to complete tasks. Part of the current Intelligence Index.

name: τ²-Bench Telecom
description: Conversational agent benchmark against a telecom support API — tool use + multi-turn dialogue. Replaced τ-bench airline/retail in AA's current suite.

variants.label: Telecom
variants.notes: AA's reported run on the telecom domain.

name: HumanEval
description: Legacy function-completion benchmark. Saturated — included only for historical continuity, not tracked by AA any more.

variants.label: pass@1
variants.notes: Single sample. Most frontier models above 95% — use with care.

name: BrowseComp
description: Web browsing/research agent benchmark.

variants.label: Default
variants.notes: Vendor-reported in Kimi K2.6 release post. Numbers for competitors are Kimi's own re-evaluations under the same conditions — may not match official reports.

variants.label: With context mgmt
variants.notes: Vendor's agent scaffold with context management.

name: DeepSearchQA
description: Deep-search QA benchmark. Reported as f1-score.

variants.label: Default
variants.notes: Vendor-reported in Kimi K2.6 release post. Numbers for competitors are Kimi's own re-evaluations under the same conditions — may not match official reports.

name: Toolathlon
description: Multi-tool agent benchmark.

variants.label: Default
variants.notes: Vendor-reported in Kimi K2.6 release post. Numbers for competitors are Kimi's own re-evaluations under the same conditions — may not match official reports.

name: OSWorld-Verified
description: Verified subset of OSWorld (computer-use agent).

variants.label: Default
variants.notes: Vendor-reported in Kimi K2.6 release post. Numbers for competitors are Kimi's own re-evaluations under the same conditions — may not match official reports.

name: Terminal-Bench 2.0
description: Terminal-Bench v2 (Terminus-2). Shell-agent tasks.

variants.label: Default
variants.notes: Vendor-reported in Kimi K2.6 release post. Numbers for competitors are Kimi's own re-evaluations under the same conditions — may not match official reports.

name: SWE-Bench Pro
description: Harder SWE-Bench split with more complex issues.

variants.label: Default
variants.notes: Vendor-reported in Kimi K2.6 release post. Numbers for competitors are Kimi's own re-evaluations under the same conditions — may not match official reports.

name: SWE-Bench Multilingual
description: SWE-Bench across multiple programming languages.

variants.label: Default
variants.notes: Vendor-reported in Kimi K2.6 release post. Numbers for competitors are Kimi's own re-evaluations under the same conditions — may not match official reports.

name: MathVision w/ python
description: MathVision with Python tool use.

variants.label: Default
variants.notes: Vendor-reported in Kimi K2.6 release post. Numbers for competitors are Kimi's own re-evaluations under the same conditions — may not match official reports.

name: V* w/ python
description: V* visual reasoning with Python tool use.

variants.label: Default
variants.notes: Vendor-reported in Kimi K2.6 release post. Numbers for competitors are Kimi's own re-evaluations under the same conditions — may not match official reports.

name: MMLU
description: Original MMLU (57 subjects, multi-task language understanding). Mostly saturated on frontier models.

name: MMMU-Pro
description: Harder multimodal understanding benchmark.

variants.label: With tools
variants.notes: Model has code execution / browsing. Not comparable to default.

name: Video-MMMU
description: Knowledge acquisition from videos.

name: SWE-Bench Verified
description: Human-verified subset of SWE-Bench — resolve real GitHub issues with code edits.

variants.label: Vendor scaffold
variants.notes: Vendor's own agent scaffold — not cross-vendor comparable.

name: ARC-AGI-2
description: Abstraction and Reasoning Corpus v2 — visual puzzle benchmark designed to resist memorization.

variants.label: Default (verified)
variants.notes: ARC Prize Verified run, no tools.

variants.label: With code execution
variants.notes: Model runs generated code to probe solutions.

name: MathArena Apex
description: Contamination-resistant math-contest benchmark from MathArena.

name: HLE — Deep Think variants
description: HLE scores from vendors' extended-reasoning / deep-think modes.

name: HMMT Feb 2025
description: Harvard-MIT Mathematics Tournament, Feb 2025 sitting.

name: HMMT Nov 2025
description: Harvard-MIT Mathematics Tournament, Nov 2025 sitting.

name: IMO AnswerBench
description: International Mathematical Olympiad answer-checking benchmark.

name: Codeforces Rating
description: Competitive-programming Codeforces Elo rating.

name: LiveCodeBench Pro
description: Competitive-programming Elo across Codeforces / ICPC / IOI (LiveCodeBench Pro).

name: BrowseComp-zh
description: Chinese-language BrowseComp.

name: MCP-Universe
description: Tool-use breadth benchmark over MCP servers.

name: MCP-Mark
description: MCP tool-use benchmark.

name: Tool-Decathlon
description: Ten-task tool-use agent benchmark.

name: GAIA
description: General AI Agent assistants benchmark from Meta FAIR et al.

name: LMArena
description: Chatbot Arena Elo rating from human preference votes.

variants.label: With thinking
variants.notes: Vendor reasoning mode on. Uses more tokens.

name: WebDev Arena
description: Web-development variant of Chatbot Arena (Elo).

name: MRCR v2
description: Multi-needle Retrieval with Context Reasoning v2 — long-context stress test.

name: CharXiv Reasoning
description: Reasoning over charts from arXiv papers.

name: ScreenSpot-Pro
description: Screen understanding benchmark.

name: OmniDocBench 1.5
description: Document OCR benchmark — overall edit distance (lower is better).

name: SimpleQA Verified
description: Vendor-verified SimpleQA factuality benchmark.

name: MMMLU
description: Multilingual MMLU.

name: Global PIQA
description: Commonsense reasoning across 100 languages/cultures.

name: FACTS Benchmark Suite
description: Grounding / parametric / multimodal / search retrieval composite.

name: Vending-Bench 2
description: Long-horizon agentic benchmark — mean net worth (USD) across runs.

name: CursorBench
description: Cursor's internal agentic coding benchmark.

name: BigLaw Bench
description: Harvey's BigLaw Bench — high-effort legal reasoning.

name: XBOW Visual Acuity
description: XBOW computer-use visual benchmark (partner-reported).

name: Finance Agent
description: Anthropic Finance Agent — general finance module.

name: Research Agent
description: Anthropic's research-agent benchmark (averaged across 6 modules).

name: WMDP Bio
description: WMDP biology — capability-probing benchmark.

name: WMDP Chem
description: WMDP chemistry — capability-probing benchmark.

name: WMDP Cyber
description: WMDP cyber — capability-probing benchmark.

name: CyBench
description: Unguided cyber-agent success rate.

name: Terminal-Bench
description: Original Terminal-Bench (predecessor to 2.0).

name: Simple-Bench
description: Multiple-choice reasoning benchmark by AI Explained where non-specialized humans outperform SOTA models. Covers spatio-temporal reasoning, social intelligence and linguistic adversarial robustness. Human baseline: 83.7%.

variants.label: AVG@5
variants.notes: Average over 5 samples, temperature 0.7 top_p 0.95 (except o1-series). Multiple-choice open-ended evaluation.

name: Aider Polyglot
description: Aider multi-language coding benchmark (polyglot). Measures pass rate on real code edits.

name: BullshitBench
description: Measures whether models clearly push back on persuasive nonsense instead of accepting or only partially challenging it, across multiple domains and bullshit techniques.

variants.label: V2 · overall green rate
variants.notes: BullshitBench V2 overall detection rate / clear-pushback rate from the public viewer. Higher means the model more reliably rejects nonsense.
